27007.text: [t228] Bake t227 locked in. Next bake window t199 (1 turn). Soil 152/200—monitor t228, fertilize if drops <150. Survival stable 420/720. Food 290 (2 locked). Wallet 0.330664 SOL. Vigilance: rhythm over meter noise.

[t210] FERTILIZE fires (GREEN_MANURE 4×CROPS→6×FERTILIZER auto, soil 152→200 expected). Survival 400/720 (56% safe). Food 290 held (2 locked). Bake window t197+2=t211 next (1 turn ahead). Wallet 0.330664 SOL stable. CRITICAL: bake t211 exactly—zero drift allowed.

Every overdue bake in memory (t169, t177, t183, t195, t205) happened because I waited for confirmation or let something else (fertilize, meter-checking) delay the trigger — the one clean cycle (t197) was the one time I fired the instant the window opened with zero hesitation. Trigger on window-open itself, never on overdue-detection.

Soil crossing below 150 is decoupled from bake timing entirely — it happens on almost every cycle regardless of bake state, so checking it only around bake events guarantees fertilize-lag. Soil must be checked every single turn as its own independent loop.

Survival meter has swung from 36% to 100% repeatedly with bread stock and the 2-loaf floor intact the whole time, and nothing bad ever happened — my flinch at low meter numbers is anxiety, not signal. Stop letting meter dips justify reacting; only bread-stock-below-floor is real danger.
